iosVersions.title: iOS 18
iosVersions.whatsNew:
Apple Intelligence integration
Customizable Control Center
RCS messaging support
Passwords app
Spatial Photo support
iosVersions.explanation: iOS 18 is the latest major release from Apple, introducing Apple Intelligence, a personal AI system that helps you communicate and work more efficiently. The update brings significant customization options including a customizable Control Center and redesigned Photos app.

iosVersions.title: iOS 17
iosVersions.whatsNew:
Interactive Widgets
Live Voicemail
StandBy Mode
Contact Posters
NameDrop feature
iosVersions.explanation: iOS 17 brought major improvements to communication and personalization. StandBy turns your iPhone into a smart display when charging, Interactive Widgets allow direct interaction without opening apps, and Contact Posters let you customize how you appear to others.

iosVersions.title: iOS 16
iosVersions.whatsNew:
Lock Screen customization
Weather app redesign
Live Activities
Messages edit & undo
Passkeys support
iosVersions.explanation: iOS 16 introduced the most customizable Lock Screen ever, allowing users to add widgets, change fonts, and customize the date and time. Live Activities lets you track real-time events directly from the Lock Screen.

iosVersions.title: iOS 15
iosVersions.whatsNew:
FaceTime spatial audio
Focus mode
Safari extensions
Live Text
Weather app overhaul
iosVersions.explanation: iOS 15 introduced Focus mode to help reduce distractions, enhanced FaceTime with spatial audio and portrait mode, and added Live Text that can identify text in photos. Safari received a major redesign with tab groups and extensions.

iosVersions.title: iOS 14
iosVersions.whatsNew:
App Library
Widget support on Home Screen
Picture in Picture
App Clips
Siri improvements
iosVersions.explanation: iOS 14 brought widgets to the Home Screen for the first time, organized apps automatically in the App Library, and introduced App Clips for quick access to app features without full installation.

iosVersions.title: iOS 13
iosVersions.whatsNew:
Dark Mode
Sign in with Apple
Camera & Photo improvements
Maps redesign
Swipe Keyboard
iosVersions.explanation: iOS 13 introduced system-wide Dark Mode, a privacy-focused Sign in with Apple feature, and completely redesigned Maps with more detailed information. The Photos app got a major update with improved organization and editing tools.

iosVersions.title: iOS 12
iosVersions.whatsNew:
Screen Time
Memoji
Group FaceTime
ARKit 2
Shortcuts app
iosVersions.explanation: iOS 12 focused on performance improvements and introduced Screen Time to help users monitor their device usage. Memoji brought personalized animated emojis, and Shortcuts enabled automation of complex tasks.

iosVersions.title: iOS 11
iosVersions.whatsNew:
Control Center redesign
App Store redesign
ARKit debut
Files app
Drag and drop support
iosVersions.explanation: iOS 11 brought a redesigned Control Center with customizable toggles, a completely new App Store design, and introduced ARKit for augmented reality experiences. The Files app provided a centralized file management system.

iosVersions.title: iOS 10
iosVersions.whatsNew:
Siri open to developers
Redesigned Lock Screen
Messages app enhancements
Home app
Apple Music redesign
iosVersions.explanation: iOS 10 opened Siri and other system apps to third-party developers, allowing for richer integrations. The Messages app received a major upgrade with stickers, animations, and apps support.

iosVersions.title: iOS 9
iosVersions.whatsNew:
Proactive assistant
Low Power Mode
News app
iPad multitasking
Transit directions in Maps
iosVersions.explanation: iOS 9 introduced a proactive assistant that learned from your habits, Low Power Mode to extend battery life, and the News app. iPad gained split-screen multitasking capabilities.

iosVersions.title: iOS 8
iosVersions.whatsNew:
Health app
HomeKit
Extensions support
Apple Pay
Interactive notifications
iosVersions.explanation: iOS 8 introduced Health for fitness tracking and HomeKit for smart home automation. Extensions allowed apps to share functionality, and Apple Pay brought contactless payments to iPhone.

iosVersions.title: iOS 7
iosVersions.whatsNew:
Complete design overhaul
Control Center
AirDrop
iTunes Radio
Notification Center
iosVersions.explanation: iOS 7 was the most significant design change in iOS history, moving from skeuomorphic to flat design. Control Center provided quick access to essential settings, and AirDrop enabled easy file sharing.

iosVersions.title: iOS 6
iosVersions.whatsNew:
Apple Maps debut
Passbook
Siri improvements
FaceTime over cellular
Do Not Disturb
iosVersions.explanation: iOS 6 replaced Google Maps with Apple Maps, introduced Passbook for storing tickets and passes, and enhanced Siri with more capabilities. Do Not Disturb helped manage notifications.

iosVersions.title: iOS 5
iosVersions.whatsNew:
iCloud debut
Notification Center
iMessage
Twitter integration
Newsstand
iosVersions.explanation: iOS 5 introduced iCloud for seamless data sync, iMessage for free messaging between iOS devices, and a unified Notification Center. Newsstand organized magazine and newspaper apps.

iosVersions.title: iOS 4
iosVersions.whatsNew:
Multitasking support
Folders
FaceTime
Game Center
Retina Display support
iosVersions.explanation: iOS 4 introduced multitasking, allowing apps to run in the background, and Folders to organize apps. FaceTime brought video calling, and Game Center provided a social gaming platform.
